items.title: Cloud credentials directory created without mode 0700 — API tokens world-listable on multi-user systems
items.intent: The cloud credentials directory must not be listable by other users — it stores API tokens and must be created with mode 0700 so directory contents are not exposed, matching the protection applied to credentials.ts file itself.
items.description: src/cloud/provisioning/credentials.ts line 53 calls mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) without a mode option when creating the cloud/ directory. This creates the directory with default permissions (0755), making it world-readable. While credentials.json is written with mode 0600 (correct), any user on the system can ls the cloud/ directory to enumerate that cloud credentials exist. Same pattern: registry.ts line 57 and snapshot-registry.ts line 51 both call mkdirSync without mode. registry.ts is already covered by BUG-077. snapshot-registry.ts stores non-sensitive data (provider, region, version, timestamp) but should be consistent. Fix: pass { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 } to all three mkdirSync calls.
steps: Read src/cloud/provisioning/credentials.ts lines 50-55 — confirm mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) has no mode option
Change mkdirSync in credentials.ts to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 })
Read src/cloud/provisioning/snapshot-registry.ts lines 48-53 — confirm mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) has no mode option
Change mkdirSync in snapshot-registry.ts to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 })
Run credentials tests and snapshot-registry tests — confirm both pass with no regressions
acceptanceCriteria: The cloud/ directory (deployDir/cloud/) is created with mode 0700 — stat confirms no group/other read bits
snapshot-registry parent directory is created with mode 0700
File permissions on credentials.json and snapshots.json remain unchanged at 0600
items.groomingNotes: RE-GROOMED 2026-03-23 (attempt 3 — source verified, line numbers confirmed). ⚠️ DEPENDENCY: BUG-084 must ship first. BUG-084 fixes scaffold.ts (root cause) and adds chmodSync to retroactively fix existing installs. After BUG-084 ships, BUG-078 is defense-in-depth — credentials.ts and snapshot-registry.ts still need mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET as belt-and-suspenders for any code path where they run before scaffold. Source read 2026-03-23: credentials.ts is 171 lines, snapshot-registry.ts is 128 lines. EXACT CHANGES REQUIRED (2 source files + 2 test files): FILE 1: src/cloud/provisioning/credentials.ts — Line 13 (verified): change 'import { FILE_MODE_SECRET }' to 'import { DIR_MODE_SECRET, FILE_MODE_SECRET }'. Line 53 (verified): change 'mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })' to 'mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET })'. FILE 2: src/cloud/provisioning/snapshot-registry.ts — After line 13 (last existing import): add 'import { DIR_MODE_SECRET } from "../../config/defaults.js";'. Line 51 (verified): change 'mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })' to 'mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET })'. BEFORE WRITING TESTS: Grep credentials.ts and snapshot-registry.ts for 'export' to confirm public API. If setProviderCredential / cloudCredentialsPath / addSnapshot are not exported, identify the correct exported function names before writing the test imports. FILE 3: src/cloud/provisioning/credentials.test.ts — mkdtempSync tmpdir in beforeEach, rmSync in afterEach. Test 1: call setProviderCredential(testDir, 'digitalocean', 'test-token'), statSync(join(testDir, 'cloud')).mode & 0o777 === 0o700. Test 2: statSync(cloudCredentialsPath(testDir)).mode & 0o777 === 0o600. FILE 4: src/cloud/provisioning/snapshot-registry.test.ts — call addSnapshot(testDir, snapshotRecord), statSync(join(testDir, 'cloud')).mode & 0o777 === 0o700. SnapshotRecord fields: snapshotId, provider, region, builderSize, clawhqVersion, builtAt (ISO string), name. AFTER CHANGES: run npm test -- --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tail -20 to confirm all tests pass. DO NOT add chmodSync to snapshot-registry — snapshots.json is non-sensitive, only the directory needs 0700.

items.title: scaffold.ts pre-creates cloud/ and security/ directories without mode 0700 — root cause of all cloud dir permission issues
items.intent: scaffold.ts must create cloud/ and security/ with mode 0700 at install time — these dirs hold API tokens and trust-mode config. All downstream permission fixes are moot if scaffold creates these dirs world-readable first.
items.description: src/build/installer/scaffold.ts scaffoldDirs() creates the full ~/.clawhq/ directory tree at install time. SCAFFOLD_DIRS includes 'security' and 'cloud' but both mkdirSync calls (line 57 for root, line 63 for subdirs) use no mode option, creating dirs at default 0755. Since mkdirSync with recursive:true does NOT change the mode of already-existing directories, all the per-file fixes (BUG-078, BUG-081, BUG-082, BUG-083) are ineffective on systems where scaffold has already run — cloud/ stays at 0755 regardless of what credentials.ts or discovery.ts pass. Fix: split SCAFFOLD_DIRS_SECRET = ['security', 'cloud'] and create those with { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET }, all others with no mode. Also apply chmodSync to security/ and cloud/ after creation to fix existing installs. Import DIR_MODE_SECRET from config/defaults.js.
items.groomingNotes: GROOMED 2026-03-23. Source verified: scaffold.ts is 103 lines. SCAFFOLD_DIRS (lines 25-42) lists 'security' and 'cloud' among 14 subdirs. All dirs created at line 63 with mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) — no mode. Root dir created at line 57 same way. Node.js mkdirSync with recursive:true does not change mode on existing dirs, so downstream per-file fixes (BUG-078, etc.) are moot if scaffold has already run. ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) scaffoldDirs() creates cloud/ and security/ with mode 0700 — stat.mode & 0o777 === 0o700. (2) All other scaffold dirs (e.g. engine, workspace, ops) are NOT created at 0700 — they use default 0755. (3) chmodSync is applied to cloud/ and security/ after creation so existing installs that already have these dirs at 0755 are corrected on next scaffoldDirs() run. (4) New scaffold.test.ts: call scaffoldDirs(mkdtempSync tmpDir), assert cloud/ at 0700, security/ at 0700, engine at 0755. IMPLEMENTATION: (1) Add 'import { DIR_MODE_SECRET } from "../../config/defaults.js";' to scaffold.ts (no existing defaults import). (2) Create two typed subsets of SCAFFOLD_DIRS: SCAFFOLD_DIRS_SECRET = ['security', 'cloud'] and SCAFFOLD_DIRS_NORMAL = all others. (3) Create secret dirs with { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET }, normal dirs with { recursive: true }. (4) Add chmodSync for security/ and cloud/ after creation to handle existing installs. (5) Verify with statSync assertions in a new scaffold.test.ts: mkdtempSync temp, call scaffoldDirs(temp), statSync(join(temp, 'cloud')).mode & 0o777 === 0o700, statSync(join(temp, 'security')).mode & 0o777 === 0o700. ALSO check that a normal dir (e.g. 'engine') does NOT have 0700 — should be 0755. Run npm test to verify no regressions.

items.title: Command queue, trust-mode switch, and heartbeat reporter create cloud/ directory without mode 0700
items.intent: cloud/ directory must be created with mode 0700 by every code path that might create it first — if any of these three files runs before credentials.ts, the cloud/ dir lands at 0755 and all downstream credential writes into it are world-listable.
items.description: Three files in src/cloud/ create the cloud/ subdirectory without a mode option: (1) src/cloud/commands/queue.ts line 61: mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) — stores commands.json with signed command payloads. (2) src/cloud/trust-modes/switch.ts line 67: mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) — stores trust-mode.json with security config. (3) src/cloud/heartbeat/reporter.ts line 63: mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) — stores heartbeat.json. All three write to deployDir/cloud/. Fix for each: add DIR_MODE_SECRET to imports from config/defaults.js, change mkdirSync to pass mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET. queue.ts already imports from defaults.js (CLOUD_COMMAND_QUEUE_MAX_HISTORY) so just add DIR_MODE_SECRET to that import. switch.ts has no defaults.js import — add one. reporter.ts already imports CLOUD_HEARTBEAT_RPC_TIMEOUT_MS from defaults.js — add DIR_MODE_SECRET to that import. No new test files needed — cloud.test.ts already tests the cloud/ directory indirectly; add dir mode assertion there.
items.groomingNotes: GROOMED 2026-03-23. Source verified. ⚠️ TEST FILE CONFLICT: BUG-086 also creates src/cloud/commands/queue.test.ts. If BUG-086 runs first, queue.test.ts already exists — add dir mode assertion to it rather than creating a new file. ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) queue.ts creates cloud/ dir with mode 0700 — stat.mode & 0o777 === 0o700. (2) switch.ts creates cloud/ dir with mode 0700 — stat.mode & 0o777 === 0o700. (3) reporter.ts creates cloud/ dir with mode 0700 — stat.mode & 0o777 === 0o700. (4) At least one test verifies cloud/ dir mode for one of these three paths (queue.test.ts preferred). THREE FILE CHANGES: (1) src/cloud/commands/queue.ts line 13: add DIR_MODE_SECRET to existing defaults import — change 'import { CLOUD_COMMAND_QUEUE_MAX_HISTORY }' to 'import { CLOUD_COMMAND_QUEUE_MAX_HISTORY, DIR_MODE_SECRET }'. Line 61: change mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET }). (2) src/cloud/trust-modes/switch.ts: no existing defaults.js import — add 'import { DIR_MODE_SECRET } from "../../config/defaults.js";' near top with other imports. Line 67: change mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET }). (3) src/cloud/heartbeat/reporter.ts line 16: add DIR_MODE_SECRET to existing defaults import — change 'import { CLOUD_HEARTBEAT_RPC_TIMEOUT_MS }' to 'import { CLOUD_HEARTBEAT_RPC_TIMEOUT_MS, DIR_MODE_SECRET }'. Line 63: change mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET }). TEST: If queue.test.ts does not exist, create it following registry.test.ts pattern (mkdtempSync/rmSync). Look at public API in queue.ts for a function that triggers directory creation without network calls — enqueueCommand() if exported. Assert statSync(join(testDir, 'cloud')).mode & 0o777 === 0o700. NOTE: writeQueueState is private — if enqueueCommand() requires external deps, use commandQueuePath() to verify path structure instead and check dir mode separately. Run npm test to verify no regressions.

items.title: commands.json written without mode 0600 — signed command payloads world-readable
items.intent: commands.json must be created with mode 0600 — it contains signed command payloads that reveal what cloud operations have been approved or rejected. World-readable on multi-user systems.
items.description: src/cloud/commands/queue.ts line 69: writeFileSync(tmpPath, content) has no mode option. commands.json stores the full command queue state including pending signed commands with their payloads and command history. Signed command payloads may contain operation details, approved action arguments, and version data. Compare with credentials.ts (uses FILE_MODE_SECRET) and registry.ts (uses FILE_MODE_SECRET via chmodSync) — commands.json has no such protection. Fix: (1) Add chmodSync and FILE_MODE_SECRET import. FILE_MODE_SECRET is already in defaults.js. queue.ts already imports CLOUD_COMMAND_QUEUE_MAX_HISTORY from defaults.js — add FILE_MODE_SECRET to that import. (2) Change writeFileSync(tmpPath, content) to writeFileSync(tmpPath, content, { mode: FILE_MODE_SECRET }) and add chmodSync(tmpPath, FILE_MODE_SECRET) before renameSync to ensure mode is applied even if umask overrides. Test: add file mode assertion to command queue tests (check src/cloud/ for existing queue tests first).
items.groomingNotes: GROOMED 2026-03-23. Source verified: queue.ts writeQueueState() at line 69 calls writeFileSync(tmpPath, content) with no mode. Public function enqueueCommand() (line 87) triggers writeQueueState — use this for testing. SignedCommand type (from src/cloud/types.ts): { id: string, type: CloudCommandType, payload?: Record<string, unknown>, createdAt: string, signature: string }. CloudCommandType includes 'health-check', 'config-patch', 'update-version', etc. ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) commands.json exists with mode 0600 — stat.mode & 0o777 === 0o600, no group/other read bits. (2) chmodSync applied before renameSync ensures mode holds even when umask overrides writeFileSync mode flag. (3) New queue.test.ts: call enqueueCommand(testDir, testCommand), assert statSync(commandQueuePath(testDir)).mode & 0o777 === 0o600. IMPLEMENTATION CHANGES to queue.ts: (1) Add chmodSync to existing node:fs import at line 10. (2) Add FILE_MODE_SECRET to existing defaults.js import at line 13 (currently imports CLOUD_COMMAND_QUEUE_MAX_HISTORY). (3) Change writeFileSync at line 69 to writeFileSync(tmpPath, content, { mode: FILE_MODE_SECRET }). (4) Add chmodSync(tmpPath, FILE_MODE_SECRET) immediately after writeFileSync and before renameSync — ensures mode applied even if umask overrides. CREATE src/cloud/commands/queue.test.ts: mkdtempSync in beforeEach, rmSync in afterEach. Import { enqueueCommand, commandQueuePath } from './queue.js'. Build test command: { id: 'cmd-1', type: 'health-check', createdAt: new Date().toISOString(), signature: 'fake-sig' }. Call enqueueCommand(testDir, testCommand). Assert statSync(commandQueuePath(testDir)).mode & 0o777 === 0o600. Do NOT assert cloud/ dir mode (that is BUG-084 scope). Run npm test to verify no regressions.

items.title: trust-mode.json and heartbeat.json written without mode 0600 — security config and health state world-readable
items.intent: trust-mode.json and heartbeat.json must be created with mode 0600 — trust-mode.json reveals the agent security posture and heartbeat.json reveals agent activity patterns. Both must be protected the same way credentials.json is.
items.description: src/cloud/trust-modes/switch.ts line 75: writeFileSync(tmpPath, content) has no mode option. trust-mode.json stores the agent trust mode (Paranoid/Zero-Trust/Managed) — revealing the security posture on a multi-user system allows an attacker to know whether the agent is configured to auto-approve cloud operations. src/cloud/heartbeat/reporter.ts line 71: writeFileSync(tmpPath, content) has no mode option. heartbeat.json stores health status and timing data. Both files follow the same write-to-tmp-then-rename pattern as commands.json (BUG-086) but without FILE_MODE_SECRET protection. Fix pattern is identical to BUG-086: add chmodSync import, add FILE_MODE_SECRET to existing defaults.js import, change writeFileSync to include { mode: FILE_MODE_SECRET }, and add chmodSync before renameSync. switch.ts has no existing defaults.js import — add one alongside the DIR_MODE_SECRET import added by BUG-085. reporter.ts already imports CLOUD_HEARTBEAT_RPC_TIMEOUT_MS from defaults.js — add FILE_MODE_SECRET there.
items.groomingNotes: GROOMED 2026-03-23. Source verified 2026-03-23: switch.ts line 75 calls writeFileSync(tmpPath, content) with no mode. reporter.ts line 71 calls writeFileSync(tmpPath, content) with no mode. Neither imports chmodSync or FILE_MODE_SECRET. DEPENDENCY: BUG-085 must run first — it adds DIR_MODE_SECRET import to switch.ts and reporter.ts. This item adds FILE_MODE_SECRET to the same imports, so the two changes are compatible. ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) trust-mode.json is created with mode 0600 — stat.mode & 0o777 === 0o600. (2) heartbeat.json is created with mode 0600 — stat.mode & 0o777 === 0o600. (3) chmodSync applied before renameSync in both files to defeat umask override. (4) At least one test for each file verifying file mode. IMPLEMENTATION FILE 1: src/cloud/trust-modes/switch.ts — BUG-085 adds 'import { DIR_MODE_SECRET } from "../../config/defaults.js";'. This item: change that import to 'import { DIR_MODE_SECRET, FILE_MODE_SECRET } from "../../config/defaults.js";'. Also add chmodSync to the existing node:fs import. Line 75: change writeFileSync(tmpPath, content) to writeFileSync(tmpPath, content, { mode: FILE_MODE_SECRET }). Add chmodSync(tmpPath, FILE_MODE_SECRET) before renameSync. IMPLEMENTATION FILE 2: src/cloud/heartbeat/reporter.ts — BUG-085 adds DIR_MODE_SECRET to existing defaults import. This item: also add FILE_MODE_SECRET. Add chmodSync to existing node:fs import. Line 71: change writeFileSync(tmpPath, content) to writeFileSync(tmpPath, content, { mode: FILE_MODE_SECRET }). Add chmodSync(tmpPath, FILE_MODE_SECRET) before renameSync. TEST: Create src/cloud/trust-modes/switch.test.ts and src/cloud/heartbeat/reporter.test.ts if no tests exist. For switch.test.ts: find the public function that writes trust-mode.json (grep switch.ts for exported functions). For reporter.test.ts: same pattern. Assert file mode 0600. If no public function triggers the write without network calls, note in the test file and test the path computation function instead. Run npm test to verify no regressions.
